items.headline: France to host men’s basketball World Cup in 2031
items.description: France was awarded the hosting rights to the 2031 men’s tournament by governing body FIBA on Wednesday, with Japan getting the 2030 Women’s World Cup.

items.headline: 3 things to watch in Magic-Pistons Game 2
items.description: Detroit is hoping to match Orlando's physical intensity and play better in the paint as Game 2 approaches.

items.headline: 3 things to watch in Thunder-Suns Game 2
items.description: Stopping Jalen Williams, plus ball movement and the play of a shooter like Grayson Allen could be key for the Suns in Game 2.

items.headline: Starting 5: Portland & Philly forge ties, Lakers go up 2-0
items.description: The Lakers take a 2-0 series edge while the 76ers and Blazers tie up their series, plus we look ahead to Wednesday's slate.

items.headline: 4 takeaways: LeBron James defies age as Lakers limit Kevin Durant to take 2-0 lead
items.description: The King refuses to let age slow him down and L.A.'s backcourt comes up big to help maintain home-court advantage.

items.headline: 4 takeaways: Scoot Henderson emerges while the Spurs stumble without Victor Wembanyama
items.description: Portland takes advantage of Victor Wembayama's absence, leaning on Scoot Henderson's playoff career-high 31 to rally.

items.headline: Utah Jazz announce return of Salt Lake City Summer League
items.description: Four teams to compete July 4, 6, and 7. Ticket sales begin in May

items.headline: 4 takeaways: Philadelphia backcourt delivers big to even series
items.description: Tyrese Maxey and VJ Edgecombe combine for 59 points and Philly crushes Boston from deep in a 111-97 Game 2 victory.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama leaves early with potential concussion
items.description: Victor Wembanyama was fouled by Jrue Holiday after he spun around the Trail Blazers point guard in the paint.

items.headline: Recap: 76ers, Trail Blazers take home court advantage, tie series 1-1
items.description: Check out the best of Tuesday's 2026 NBA Playoffs action with the NBA.com live blog.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander named 2025-26 Kia NBA Clutch Player of the Year
items.description: The Thunder superstar led the NBA with 175 total clutch points and 6.5 points per game in clutch time.

items.headline: The Athletic: Before Hardaway Jr. found the ‘perfect fit’ he had to embrace his mistakes
items.description: The Sixth Man contender is flourishing in Denver and sees himself as a testament to humility, perseverance and vulnerability.

items.headline: The Athletic: Rudy Gobert stands taller than ever in Game 2 showdown
items.description: Gobert knows how many NBA fans belittle him. But the Minnesota center proved his value in bottling up Jokić in the fourth quarter.

items.headline: Coach Billy Donovan steps down as Bulls' coach after 6 seasons
items.description: The Bulls announced Billy Donovan, 60, is stepping aside as coach after spending the past 6 seasons with the team.

items.headline: Starting 5: A pair of wild comebacks, Cavs roll on & look at Tuesday's trio of games
items.description: The Wolves and Hawks get comeback wins, the Cavs keep rolling and we look ahead to Tuesday's trio of Game 2s, too.
